P001:
History: 58-year-old man with type 2 diabetes mellitus (dx 2016) and essential hypertension. Prior admission for community-acquired pneumonia in 2022.
Medications: metformin 1000 mg twice daily; lisinopril 10 mg once daily; atorvastatin 20 mg at night.
Allergies: penicillin (urticaria).
P002:
History: 34-year-old woman with intermittent asthma since childhood and seasonal allergic rhinitis.
Medications: salbutamol inhaler as needed; cetirizine 10 mg during pollen season.
Allergies: no known drug allergies.
P003:
History: 71-year-old woman with atrial fibrillation, chronic kidney disease stage 3, and osteoarthritis of both knees.
Medications: apixaban 5 mg twice daily; bisoprolol 2.5 mg once daily; paracetamol as needed.
Allergies: codeine (nausea and vomiting).
